fields.definition_origin: Conversion funnel analysis traces to early direct-marketing analysis (Daniel Starch 1923 'Principles of Advertising') and was applied to web analytics by Avinash Kaushik's 'Web Analytics: An Hour a Day' (Wiley 2007) and 'Web Analytics 2.0' (2009). Cohort analysis as a discrete technique was named in software-product context by Eric Ries / David Skok ('cohort analysis is the most important metric for SaaS' — David Skok, forentrepreneurs.com 2011) and codified in 'Lean Analytics' (Croll & Yoskovitz, O'Reilly 2013). LTV (Lifetime Value) modeling for SaaS popularized by David Skok and Sean Ellis (2010-2014). Mobile-onboarding-specific application matured via RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps reports (annual since 2021), Adapty Paywall Benchmarks (annual since 2022), AppsFlyer / Adjust attribution-and-LTV reports (2018-2026), and FunnelFox / Funnel.io best-practice articles (blog.funnelfox.com 2024-2026 'Mobile App Analytics Best Practices').
fields.core_mechanism: Funnel analysis identifies WHERE users drop off in a sequence of intended steps (install → onboarding_started → step_completed_1 → ... → paywall_shown → trial_started → subscribed → renewed). Each step's conversion rate (% of prior step) reveals surgical optimization targets — a 22% drop on step 7 isolates exactly which copy / question / animation needs work. Cohort analysis groups users by a shared attribute (install month, acquisition channel, experiment variant, archetype) and tracks their behavior over time — revealing whether retention/LTV is improving, decaying, or oscillating with channel quality. LTV (lifetime value) is the only honest north-star metric: DAU/MAU mask quality (you can buy DAU with low-LTV users); 30-day or 90-day or 12-month LTV reflects whether the acquired user is actually valuable. Cohort LTV TREND is more useful than absolute LTV — a declining trend signals acquisition-quality decay (your TikTok ads are now reaching less-qualified users). For onboarding specifically, cohort-by-experiment-variant LTV is the optimization unit: 'cohort A saw onboarding_v3, has $54.50 12mo LTV; cohort B saw v2, $7.40' is the direct optimization signal. Without these three (funnel + cohort + LTV), conversion teams optimize on noise.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Adapty 2026 'High-Performing Paywall' report: weekly + 3-day-trial flow → $54.50 12mo LTV vs $7.40 without (636% lift), measured cohort-by-cohort. (2) Adapty 2026: top performers run 14.7 paywall experiments/yr; cumulative LTV gap vs non-testing teams up to 40x. (3) RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2025/2026: cited cohort LTV as the canonical experimentation metric. (4) David Skok's SaaS metrics framework (forentrepreneurs.com 2010-2024): LTV / CAC ratio target >3 for healthy SaaS. (5) Lean Analytics (Croll & Yoskovitz 2013): cohort analysis as 'the most important spreadsheet you'll ever build.' (6) AppsFlyer 2024 mobile-attribution benchmarks: subscription-app D7 ROAS varies 5x across channels, cohort segmentation by channel mandatory. (7) FunnelFox 2024-2026 'Mobile App Analytics Best Practices': practitioner walkthrough of funnel + cohort setup. (8) Mixpanel and Amplitude both ship pre-built funnel + cohort + LTV reports. (9) PostHog 2024 funnel-builder includes cohort overlay. (10) Specific RCT-grade evidence is rare; most evidence is industry benchmark + practitioner case study.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Per-step conversion rate (step N → step N+1), funnel completion rate (install → subscribed), cohort retention curves (D1, D7, D30, D90), cohort LTV at fixed horizons (D30, D90, 12mo), LTV/CAC ratio, payback period, churn rate per cohort, 30-day-paid retention, trial-to-paid conversion, ad-channel cohort LTV.
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (decades of practice consensus; specific lift figures vary by app)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Adapty 2026: 636% LTV lift weekly+3-day-trial vs no trial. Top performers' cumulative experiment-driven LTV gap up to 40x vs non-testing teams. Cal AI's $30M Y1 ARR derives from sustained funnel + cohort optimization (Mobbin teardowns + investor pitches).
fields.engineering_tactics:
(1) FUNNEL DEFINITION: in PostHog/Mixpanel/Amplitude, define onboarding funnel as ordered sequence: install → onboarding_started → onboarding_step_completed_1 → ... → onboarding_completed → paywall_shown → trial_started OR purchased → trial_active_d3 → trial_converted_to_paid → renewed_month_2 → renewed_month_6 → renewed_month_12. Visualize per-step conversion. Time window per step: 7 days default; tune. (2) COHORT GROUPING: by install_month (year-month), acquisition_channel (TikTok / Meta / organic / referral / Google Ads — propagated via attribution SDK), experiment_variant (item 45), archetype (item 52), locale, ad_creative_id. (3) LTV CALCULATION: ARPU_per_cohort × retention_curve_per_cohort, summed across N months. RevenueCat / Adapty / Glassbox provide pre-built cohort LTV. Hand-rolled: SQL on Postgres / BigQuery / Snowflake. EXAMPLE LTV query (simplified):
```sql
WITH cohort_arpu AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', u.install_date) AS cohort_month,
    u.acquisition_channel,
    u.experiment_variant,
    u.user_id,
    SUM(p.amount_usd) FILTER (
      WHERE p.created_at <= u.install_date + INTERVAL '30 days'
    ) AS ltv_d30,
    SUM(p.amount_usd) AS ltv_total
  FROM users u
  LEFT JOIN payments p ON p.user_id = u.user_id
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4
)
SELECT
  cohort_month,
  acquisition_channel,
  experiment_variant,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS n_users,
  AVG(ltv_d30) AS avg_ltv_d30,
  AVG(ltv_total) AS avg_ltv_to_date
FROM cohort_arpu
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
ORDER BY cohort_month DESC;
```
(4) RETENTION CURVES: % of cohort active on D1, D7, D14, D30, D60, D90 — visualize as decay curves; healthy curves flatten (sticky users); unhealthy curves continue declining. (5) UNIT ECONOMICS: LTV/CAC ratio per cohort + channel; payback period (months until cumulative ARPU = CAC). Target LTV/CAC >3, payback <12 months for healthy SaaS. (6) ATTRIBUTION: AppsFlyer / Adjust / Branch.io for ad-source attribution; pass acquisition_channel + ad_creative_id as superproperties for cohort segmentation. SKAdNetwork (iOS post-IDFA) for privacy-preserving attribution. (7) DASHBOARD: per-cohort LTV-by-channel grid, refreshed daily — the single most-important dashboard for paid-acquisition apps. (8) SAMPLE-SIZE GATES: cohort < 100 users → noisy; flag insufficient sample. (9) ATTRIBUTION-DECAY MODEL: weekly cohort LTV stable 12+ weeks post-install; daily cohort LTV requires longer observation. (10) PREDICTIVE LTV: ML model trained on early-engagement signals (D7 retention, paywall_view, trial_start) predicts D365 LTV with reasonable accuracy after ~3 months of cohort observation; useful for early experiment decisions. (11) WIN/LOSS DEFINITION for experiments: pre-register the metric and the threshold (e.g., '+10% trial-to-paid at p<0.05'); avoid p-hacking by post-hoc cohort slicing. (12) STATISTICAL VALIDITY: PostHog/Statsig provide built-in statistical engines (CUPED, sequential testing); honor sample-size and runtime requirements before declaring winner.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI: cohort analysis by ad-creative-id drove iteration on TikTok creative, scaling from $0 to $30M ARR Y1. (2) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: cohort retention curves drove the 5min→15min onboarding extension decision (his tracked cohorts post-extension showed 3x organic conversion). (3) Adapty 2026 customer dashboards: cohort LTV by trial-vs-no-trial documented 636% lift. (4) Duolingo's public engineering blog: heavy cohort analysis informs streak-design + notification copy. (5) RevenueCat dashboards (default product surface): cohort LTV per acquisition source is the marketed view. (6) Headspace / Headway / Noom — all run cohort + funnel + experiment analyses (visible from team-job-postings descriptions). (7) AppsFlyer Performance Index reports per-channel D7-D30 ROAS for top apps. (8) Counter-example failure: pre-2022 indie subscription apps relying on absolute MRR / DAU / MAU grew to $5K MRR then plateaued — couldn't see cohort-quality decay until churn surged. (9) Croll & Yoskovitz (Lean Analytics) document multiple case studies of cohort analysis reframing PM priorities.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-specific challenges: iOS post-IDFA attribution requires SKAdNetwork (delayed, aggregated, lossy); cross-device LTV (item 33) requires same-user-id attribution. Web simpler — cookie-based attribution + first-party tracking. Mobile-specific events: install_attribution, sk_ad_network_postback, app_installed, app_opened. Cross-platform: same cohort definitions; SDK differs.
fields.category_fit: MANDATORY for any subscription app, paid-acquisition app, or category with cohort-quality risk. Strongest leverage in high-CAC mobile (wellness, AI-wrapper, fintech) where every dollar of acquisition needs cohort-LTV proof. Skippable for: free utility apps with no acquisition spend.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. PostHog or RevenueCat pre-built funnel + cohort dashboards usable in 1-2 days. Hand-rolled SQL on warehouse: 1-2 days. Attribution SDK integration (AppsFlyer / Adjust): 1 day. Solo dev should leverage RevenueCat (free up to $2.5K MTR) which auto-builds cohort LTV.
fields.tooling_options: FUNNEL + COHORT: PostHog (free 1M events, includes funnels + cohorts + retention), Mixpanel (free 100K MTU, mature funnel UX), Amplitude (free 10M events, mature cohort analysis). LTV / SUBSCRIPTION: RevenueCat (free up to $2.5K MTR, marketed for cohort LTV), Adapty (free tier, paywall analytics), Glassbox, Superwall. ATTRIBUTION: AppsFlyer (paid, comprehensive), Adjust (paid), Branch.io (free tier limited). SKADNETWORK: AppsFlyer / Adjust handle iOS-side. WAREHOUSING: BigQuery / Snowflake / Redshift / Postgres for ad-hoc SQL. EXPERIMENT ENGINES: PostHog Experiments, Statsig, Eppo, Optimizely, LaunchDarkly Experimentation. PREDICTIVE LTV: Hightouch / Census + custom ML model. VISUALIZATION: Metabase / Looker / Mode.
fields.ai_dependency: none for the analysis. Optional: AI-driven anomaly detection on cohort dashboards (Anodot, custom GPT integrations).
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to author cohort + funnel queries. Prompt fragment 1: 'Author SQL queries on Supabase Postgres that compute: (1) onboarding funnel by experiment_variant — count of distinct users at each step (started, step_1_completed, step_5_completed, step_10_completed, paywall_shown, trial_started, trial_converted, renewed_month_2). (2) cohort retention curve — for users who installed in 2026-04, % active on D1, D7, D14, D30. (3) cohort LTV — sum of payments by cohort_month + acquisition_channel + experiment_variant, averaged. Output queries with comments explaining join logic. Build an Edge Function that runs daily and writes into a cohort_summary table consumed by the admin dashboard.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Implement attribution: integrate AppsFlyer SDK on RN, capture install attribution + ad_creative_id, propagate as superproperty on every event (item 43). Cross-reference with PostHog cohorts.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Audit the funnel — flag any step with sample size <100 in last 28 days, flag any drop-off >40% as optimization target, flag any cohort showing declining LTV trend over 4 consecutive months as acquisition-quality alert.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to ad-hoc SELECT COUNT(*) queries without cohort grouping. AI computes 'conversion rate' as overall total without segmentation. AI uses GROUP BY user_id producing one-row-per-user instead of cohort aggregation. AI averages amounts ignoring outliers (use MEDIAN or trimmed mean). AI ignores attribution. Counter-prompt: 'Compute cohort LTV by (cohort_month, acquisition_channel, experiment_variant) — never overall total. Use AVG(SUM(...)) two-step aggregation per user, then per cohort. Filter out test accounts. Include sample size N per cohort. Flag insufficient (N<100) cohorts as null. Use SKAdNetwork-aggregated attribution on iOS post-IDFA, AppsFlyer install_attribution otherwise.' AI also defaults to 'first-touch attribution' without disclosure — counter: 'document attribution model (first-touch / last-touch / multi-touch) explicitly; reconcile with revenue data.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Vanity metrics (DAU/MAU absolute) without cohort segmentation — masks quality decay. (2) p-hacking via post-hoc cohort slicing — declare metric pre-experiment. (3) Sample sizes <100 declared as 'directional' — usually noise. (4) LTV without payback period — buying users at LTV/CAC >3 with payback >24 months still bankrupts you. (5) Single-channel attribution (last-touch only) — under-credits early-funnel channels. (6) Ignoring SKAdNetwork lossiness on iOS — iOS LTV-by-channel less precise than Android post-IDFA. (7) Average-only LTV — ignore distribution; whales distort mean. (8) Cohort definition drift — change cohort definition mid-experiment, history breaks. (9) Funnel without time-window (7-day vs 28-day) — different windows yield different conversions. (10) Attribution conflicts (AppsFlyer says X, internal says Y) — reconcile, document. (11) Predictive LTV deployed without ground-truth validation — overfits.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. (a) GDPR Art. 13 transparency — declare LTV/cohort tracking in privacy notice. (b) Cohort analysis on health/identity data triggers Art. 9 special-category requirements. (c) iOS ATT — IDFA-based attribution requires opt-in (44% iOS rate per 2025); use SKAdNetwork (privacy-preserving). (d) GDPR right-to-erasure — deleted users must be removed from cohort dashboards (or anonymized with k-anonymity). (e) DFA Q4 2026 — manipulative-pattern detection may require disclosing cohort experimentation; document governance. (f) COPPA 2026 — children's data shouldn't enter LTV models without verifiable parental consent.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple SKAdNetwork 4.0 required for iOS attribution post-IDFA. Apple PrivacyManifest mandates declaring tracking SDKs. Google Play Privacy Sandbox (Android, 2024-2026) restricting cross-app attribution. Apple App Store Privacy Nutrition Label declares analytics use.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Cohort observation horizon: 12-month LTV needs 12 months of data — early experiments use proxy metrics (D30 paid retention). (2) Currency conversion: report cohort LTV in USD with FX rate at install time, not realization time. (3) Refunds: subtract refunds from LTV. (4) Trial-to-paid conversion lag: trial users count as converted only after billing cycle posts. (5) Gross vs net LTV: subtract Apple/Google 30% (15% small-business), payment processing, attribution costs. (6) Geographic cohort variance: LATAM LTV is 30-50% of US LTV (item 36 localization). (7) Statistical-significance overshoot: large samples yield significant tiny effects; check practical significance. (8) Bayesian vs frequentist statistics: PostHog defaults Bayesian, Mixpanel frequentist — interpret accordingly. (9) Attribution mismatches between vendors — pick a primary, reconcile. (10) RevenueCat / Adapty are Apple/Google receipt-grounded but may differ from raw StoreKit by ~1-3% (refund timing).
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Cohort + LTV are universal techniques. Locale-specific: LTV varies by purchasing power (US/EU/JP higher; LATAM/SE Asia lower); LTV-per-locale should be tracked separately, not aggregated. Pricing localization (item 36) interacts: localized prices may yield higher LTV but lower per-unit revenue. JP cohorts may show longer trial periods. RTL doesn't affect analytics.
fields.prescribed_action: Build per-step funnel + cohort retention curves + cohort LTV dashboards from Day 1. Cohorts segmented by install_month + acquisition_channel + experiment_variant + locale + archetype. Track LTV/CAC ratio (target >3) and payback period (target <12 months). Use RevenueCat or Adapty for subscription cohort LTV; PostHog or Mixpanel for funnel; AppsFlyer for attribution. Pre-register experiment metrics; honor sample-size gates. Publish a daily-refresh dashboard. Forbid: vanity metrics without cohort, post-hoc cohort slicing, sample-size <100 'directional' claims.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Indirect — questionnaire abandonment per step feeds funnel; question-type-mix (item 27) tested via experiment_variant cohorts; identity question late in flow correlates with downstream LTV — measurable via cohort.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect — measure post-Aha (after AI plan reveal, item 12) cohort retention vs pre-Aha to validate Aha effectiveness. Cohort LTV by experiment_variant on Aha-engineering tests (loader copy, reveal animation).
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: N/A — though archetype is a recommended cohort dimension to test if archetype-conditioned variants (item 52) drive different LTV.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'vanity metrics' / 'absolute MRR worship' / 'no cohort segmentation' antipatterns. Pairs with item 43 (Tracking Plan) — events feed funnels. Pairs with item 45 (Feature Flags + A/B Test) — cohort by experiment_variant. Pairs with item 36 (Localization) — cohort by locale.
uncertain: specific aggregate conversion lift figures attributable purely to funnel/cohort/LTV adoption (effect indirect via better experimentation decisions)
